Pathway state hierarchy butterfly plot

PlotPathwayButterfly.Rd

Scores cells against four pathways, maps them to a 2-D hierarchy coordinate system, and produces a butterfly layout: one large overview panel (all cells colored by group.by) plus one small panel per group level (group highlighted in color; remaining cells in gray). When split.by is supplied, a separate figure is produced for each split level.

Usage

PlotPathwayButterfly(
  seurat_object,
  group.by,
  quadrant_order,
  split.by = NULL,
  gene_sets = NULL,
  pathway_sets = list(Hallmark = list(category = "H")),
  species = "Homo sapiens",
  quadrant_labels = NULL,
  group_colors = NULL,
  assay = "RNA",
  layer = "counts",
  subsample = FALSE,
  n_cells = 20000L,
  subsample_warn_at = 50000L,
  center = TRUE,
  log_scale = TRUE,
  overview_title = NULL,
  overview_size = 2,
  panel_ncol = NULL,
  point_size = 0.3,
  point_alpha = 0.6,
  label_size_large = 5,
  label_size_small = 2.5,
  title_size_large = 12,
  title_size_small = 8,
  width = NULL,
  height = NULL,
  file_name = NULL,
  output_dir = NULL,
  caffeinate = FALSE
)

Arguments

seurat_object

A Seurat object.

group.by

Character. Metadata column for cell grouping and coloring.

quadrant_order

Character vector of exactly four pathway names, in order: bottom-left, bottom-right, top-left, top-right. Names must match either names(gene_sets) (custom mode) or pathway names in the requested MSigDB collection.

split.by

Character or NULL. Metadata column whose levels each generate a separate butterfly figure. Default: NULL.

gene_sets

Named list of character vectors — one element per pathway. When NULL (default), gene sets are fetched from MSigDB via msigdbr using pathway_sets and filtered to quadrant_order.

pathway_sets

Named list specifying MSigDB collection(s) to search. Each element is a list with category and optional subcategory. Only used when gene_sets = NULL. Default: list(Hallmark = list(category = "H")).

species

Species string for msigdbr. Default: "Homo sapiens".

quadrant_labels

Character vector of four display labels for the corner boxes (order matches quadrant_order: bl, br, tl, tr). Defaults to auto-shortened pathway names.

group_colors

Named character vector mapping group levels to colors. Defaults to PrepObject colors or auto-generated palette.

assay

Assay to extract counts from. Default: "RNA".

layer

Layer / slot name. Default: "counts".

subsample

Logical. When TRUE, randomly sample n_cells cells before scoring. Default: FALSE.

n_cells

Integer. Number of cells to sample when subsample = TRUE. Default: 20000L.

subsample_warn_at

Integer or NULL. If the cell count exceeds this value and subsample = FALSE, a message is emitted. Set NULL to silence. Default: 50000L.

center

Logical. Column-center scores before hierarchy transform. Default: TRUE.

log_scale

Logical. Apply log2 scaling to hierarchy coordinates. Default: TRUE.

overview_title

Character. Title for the overview panel. Defaults to the object name stored by PrepObject.

overview_size

Numeric. Width of the overview panel relative to one small panel column. Default: 2.

panel_ncol

Integer or NULL. Number of columns in the small- panel grid. Defaults to ceiling(n_groups / 2).

point_size

Numeric. Point size. Default: 0.3.

point_alpha

Numeric. Point alpha. Default: 0.6.

label_size_large

Numeric. Corner label size for the overview panel. Default: 5.

label_size_small

Numeric. Corner label size for small panels. Default: 2.5.

title_size_large

Numeric. Panel title font size for the overview. Default: 12.

title_size_small

Numeric. Panel title font size for small panels. Default: 8.

width

Numeric or NULL. PDF width in inches. When NULL (default), width is auto-sized as (overview_size + panel_ncol) * 1.5.

height

Numeric or NULL. PDF height in inches. When NULL (default), height is auto-sized as panel_nrow * 2.8 + 0.4.

file_name

Character or NULL. Output file name (without directory). Defaults to "<object_name> pathway butterfly.pdf".

output_dir

Character or NULL. Directory for saving the PDF. Falls back to the PrepObject output directory. When NULL, the plot is printed to the active device.

caffeinate

Logical. Keep the machine awake during computation. Default: FALSE.

Value

Invisibly, a named list (one element per split level) each containing hierarchy (the X/Y coordinate data frame) and scores (the raw pathway score matrix, cells × pathways).

Quadrant layout

quadrant_order specifies the four pathway names in the order bottom-left, bottom-right, top-left, top-right. The Y-axis separates top from bottom; the X-axis gives the left-right balance within whichever axis dominates for each cell.

Scoring

Pathway scores are computed as the mean expression across each pathway's genes per cell (adapted from the scrabble R package). Scores are column-centered when center = TRUE (default) so that the mean score of each pathway across all cells is zero before the hierarchy transform.

On this page

Developed by Nourhan Abdelfattah.
